wine_restaurants.name: Ente
wine_restaurants.neighborhood: Pfortenhaus Kloster Eberbach, Eltville am Rhein
wine_restaurants.cuisine: Refined modern cuisine drawing on global flavours; the wine list anchors in Rheingau Rieslings with deep producer relationships
wine_restaurants.description: Hommage Hotels' Ente moved in March 2026 to the gatehouse of Kloster Eberbach during the Nassauer Hof renovation in Wiesbaden. Chef Michael Kammermeier's refined cooking with a Riesling-led list.

wine_restaurants.name: Burgrestaurant Schwarzenstein
wine_restaurants.cuisine: Classical German cooking on the Burg with a wine list led by Rheingau VDP estates
wine_restaurants.description: Castle restaurant at Relais and Chateaux Burg Schwarzenstein above Geisenheim-Johannisberg. Classical German cooking and a Rheingau-anchored wine list with vineyard-view terrace seating.

wine_restaurants.name: Brasserie Schwarzenstein
wine_restaurants.cuisine: Authentic French brasserie cuisine by Christofer Kokozska and Marco Stenger in a glazed pavilion with Rhine valley views; Rheingau-led wine list
wine_restaurants.description: Glazed-pavilion brasserie at Burg Schwarzenstein in Geisenheim-Johannisberg, French cuisine by Christofer Kokozska and Marco Stenger. Rheingau-led wine list with vineyard panoramas over the Rhine valley.

wine_restaurants.name: Restaurant Jean
wine_restaurants.cuisine: French-inspired fine dining by chef-patron Johannes Frankenbach with a wine list led by Rheingau growers
wine_restaurants.description: Eltville fine-dining destination of chef-patron Johannes Frankenbach with French-inspired tasting menus. The wine programme is led by Rheingau estates, supported by Bordeaux and Burgundy depth.

wine_restaurants.name: Restaurant Kronenschloesschen
wine_restaurants.cuisine: Modern French tasting menus by chef Marcus Langer, with the Gault Millau Beste Weinkarte Deutschlands wine list led by Head Sommelier Florian Richter

awards.source: Gault Millau Best Wine List in Germany

wine_restaurants.description: Hattenheim fine-dining restaurant at the family-run 4-star Kronenschloesschen. Marcus Langer heads the kitchen since August 2025; sommelier Florian Richter, Germany's Best Sommelier 2023, runs the cellar.

wine_restaurants.name: Weinhaus Zum Krug
wine_restaurants.cuisine: Traditional Rheingau cooking with a 350-bottle wine list spanning Rheingau growers; sauerbraten, Wispertal trout and Rheingauer sausage
wine_restaurants.description: Hotel and wine house in a 1720 half-timbered building in central Hattenheim, run by the Laufer family for three generations. Around 350 Rheingau wines alongside regional plates from grandmothers' recipes.

wine_restaurants.name: Klosterschaenke Kloster Eberbach
wine_restaurants.address: Kloster Eberbach, 65346 Eltville am Rhein, Germany
wine_restaurants.cuisine: Monastery tavern cooking with regional Hessian and Cistercian-tradition plates; wine list led entirely by the Hessische Staatsweinguter Kloster Eberbach range
wine_restaurants.description: Monastery tavern at the 12th-century Kloster Eberbach, the Cistercian abbey above Eltville. The list is entirely Kloster Eberbach wines from the 200-hectare estate run by the Hessische Staatsweinguter.

wine_restaurants.name: Schlossschaenke auf dem Johannisberg
wine_restaurants.cuisine: Seasonal regional cooking with Mediterranean accents; the wine list is built around Schloss Johannisberg's own Rieslings
wine_restaurants.description: Hilltop restaurant of Schloss Johannisberg above Geisenheim with conservatory and sun-terrace seating. Set menus and a la carte built around the estate's own Riesling range with views over the Rheingau.

wine_restaurants.name: Marcobrunn by Tristan Brandt
wine_restaurants.cuisine: Modern French cuisine with Asian influences; culinary direction by Simone Kubitzek and Hella Eggers under Tristan Brandt's patronage
wine_restaurants.description: Restored gourmet restaurant of Hotel Schloss Reinhartshausen in Erbach, reopened September 2025 with chefs Simone Kubitzek and Hella Eggers under Tristan Brandt. Vignette Collection by IHG.

wine_restaurants.name: Restaurant Baiken by Schroeer
wine_restaurants.cuisine: Classical fine-dining cooking by Dirk Schroeer with the Rauenthal Baiken vineyard alongside; wine list led by Rheingau Riesling
wine_restaurants.description: Restaurant in the Baiken vineyard pavilion above Rauenthal, run by Dirk Schroeer and Amila Begic-Schroeer. Classical fine-dining cooking with views over the Rheingau slopes and a Rheingau-led wine list.

wine_restaurants.name: Restaurant Lindenwirt
wine_restaurants.cuisine: Regional Rheingau cooking with a Drosselgasse wine garden; Schwaebisch Hall pasture-raised beef and local game alongside Rheingau Rieslings
wine_restaurants.description: Restaurant of the 1927 Hotel Lindenwirt on the Drosselgasse, run by the third generation of the Ohlig family. Regional cooking with Rheingau wines in the wine garden or the Wirtshaus zum Drosselmueller alongside.

wine_restaurants.name: Ruedesheimer Schloss Restaurant
wine_restaurants.cuisine: Regional Rheingau cooking in a 1729 tithe-barn courtyard; Rheingau Riesling and Assmannshaeuser Spatburgunder on the list
wine_restaurants.description: Restaurant at the Ruedesheimer Schloss on the corner of Steingasse and Drosselgasse, set in a 1729 former tithe barn. Regional Rheingau plates with the estate's Rieslings and the local Spatburgunder.

wine_restaurants.name: Heiliggeist Restaurant Mainz
wine_restaurants.cuisine: Restaurant in the 13th-century Heiliggeist former hospital with Gothic vaults; wine list spans Rheingau, Rheinhessen and Pfalz growers
wine_restaurants.description: Restaurant in the Gothic former Heiliggeist hospital in Mainz old town, with vaulted ceilings and stained glass. Rheingau, Rheinhessen and Pfalz wines paired with seasonal regional plates.

wine_restaurants.name: Weinhaus Sinz Restaurant
wine_restaurants.cuisine: Seasonal regional cooking in a half-century-old family wine house with Rheingau wines in the middle of the Wiesbaden vineyards
wine_restaurants.description: Family-run wine-house restaurant in Wiesbaden-Frauenstein in the middle of the Rheingau vineyards. Seasonal regional cooking with Wiener Schnitzel, Tafelspitz, steaks and the house Rheingau wines.
